name: Sayf ibn Malik
title: Brave of Bani Abdi
shortDescription: A Shia and warrior from Basra who joined the Imam in Mecca and was martyred in the hand-to-hand combat of Ashura.
fullStory:
Sayf ibn Malik al-Abdi was a sincere Shia and renowned warrior in Basra. He was from the Bani Abdi clan, among whom the love for the Ahl al-Bayt had deep roots. Sayf left Basra upon learning of Imam Hussain's (AS) uprising.

Traveling long routes, he joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan in Mecca and from that moment dedicated himself to serving and helping the Prophet's (PBUH) son. Sayf was a constant companion of the Imam all the way from Mecca to Karbala.

On the day of Ashura, when the flames of war ignited, Sayf ibn Malik, with a firm heart and unwavering faith, sought the Imam's permission and entered the battlefield. He was a skilled swordsman whose presence in the field troubled the enemy.

Sayf fought valiantly in hand-to-hand combat and, after killing a group of enemies, was martyred by sword strikes, making his life a shield for Wilayah.

His loyalty from Basra to Mecca and from Mecca to Karbala is clear evidence of his insight and deep love for the Prophet's (PBUH) household.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Sayf ibn Malik.
